previous_statement: Setup and spectral bounds. Phi is unital positive on B(H)_sa. By node 1.1.1, ||Phi||=1 in the order-unit norm; by GT-positive-unital, Phi(I)=I. Work in the unital normed algebra End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2). Put S:=2Phi-I; then ||S||<=2||Phi||+||I||=3 by the triangle inequality, homogeneity and ||Id||=1 (node 1.1.2). Since S^2-I=(2Phi-I)^2-I=4Phi^2-4Phi=4(Phi^2-Phi), by def almost-idempotent ||S^2-I||=4||Phi^2-Phi||<=4 eta.
new_statement: Setup and spectral bounds. Phi is unital positive on B(H)_sa. By node 1.1.1, ||Phi||=1 in the order-unit norm; Phi(I)=I by hypothesis (def positive-unital-map). Work in the unital Banach algebra End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2). Put S:=2Phi-I; then ||S||<=2||Phi||+||I||=3 by the triangle inequality, homogeneity and ||Id||=1 (node 1.1.2). Since S^2-I=(2Phi-I)^2-I=4Phi^2-4Phi=4(Phi^2-Phi), by def almost-idempotent ||S^2-I||=4||Phi^2-Phi||<=4 eta.
